data.title: BD's latest Research Cloud aids immunology and cancer research. AI tool automates panel design for better results NYSE: BDX JAN 23, 2026.
data.description: The latest update of BD® Research Cloud introduces an innovative AI-powered feature for automatic panel design aimed at enhancing the quality, efficiency, and usability of scientific outcomes across various research domains. This advancement was announced in Franklin Lakes, N.J. on January 23, 2026. BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) listed on the NYSE as BDX spearheaded this development.

data.title: Innovative Industrial Properties (IIP) details tax treatment for 2025 distributions: Total Distribution Per Share $1.90, including $1.07 taxable ordinary dividends.
data.description: In San Diego today, Innovative Industrial Properties, Inc. (IIP) (NYSE: IIPR) reported the tax treatment for its 2025 distributions. The details are as follows: Security Description: Common Stock, CUSIP: 45781V101, Ticker Symbol: IIPR. For shareholders, the Record Date was 12/31/2024 and the Payable Date is 01/15/2025. The Total Distribution Per Share was $1.900000, out of which $1.070000 is attributed to 2025 Taxable Ordinary Dividend, $0.970900 to Return of Capital, $0.099100 to Long-Term Capital Gain, and $0 to Unrecaptured Section 1250 Gain and Section 199A Dividend.

data.title: SLB reports Q4 2025 revenue of $9.745M, income before taxes of $943M. Plans to return over $4B to shareholders in 2026.
data.description: Houston headquartered SLB reported their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. Revenue for the fourth quarter was $9.745 billion, showing a 9% increase compared to the previous quarter and a 5% increase year-on-year. Income before taxes on a GAAP basis was $943 million for the quarter, reflecting a 6% decrease from the previous quarter and a 32% decrease year-on-year. The income before taxes margin on a GAAP basis was 9.7%, representing a 153 basis points sequential decrease and a 526 basis points year-on-year decrease. Net income attributable to SLB on a GAAP basis was $824 million for the quarter. These results were stated in millions, except per share amounts.

data.title: Jeffs' Brands preparing AI security solutions for 2026 FIFA World Cup across 3 countries. Expanding into homeland security.
data.description: On January 23, 2026, in Tel Aviv, Israel, Jeffs' Brands Ltd, a data-driven e-commerce company listed on Nasdaq under JFBR and JFBRW, revealed their plans to enter the global homeland security sector using advanced AI technology. They aim to assist in security operations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, set to take place in Canada, Mexico, and the United States. As the world's biggest sporting event, this presents a significant opportunity for the company to expand its reach.

data.title: Medical suction devices market to grow at 5.37% CAGR reaching USD 25.44 billion by 2031, up from USD 19.59 billion in 2026.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of January 23, 2026, the medical suction devices market has been reported to have a value of USD 19.59 billion. A recent study by Mordor Intelligence predicts that this market is expected to exceed USD 25.44 billion by 2031, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.37% between 2026 and 2031.

data.title: Advanced Ceramics Market projected to reach $17.24 billion by 2030 according to MarketsandMarkets; diverse materials and product types contribute to market growth.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida on January 23rd, 2026, data from MarketsandMarkets revealed insights on the Advanced Ceramics Market. The market is categorized by various materials including Alumina, Titanate, Zirconia, Silicon Carbide, Piezo Ceramic, and others. Product types in this market consist of Monolithic Ceramics, Ceramic Coatings, Ceramic Matrix Composites, and more.

data.title: Ericsson surpasses Q4 earnings expectations, reports EPS of 27 cents, sales of $7.37 billion, proposes bigger dividends.
data.description: Ericsson's stock went up as they announced their strong fourth-quarter earnings. Their EPS of 27 cents surpassed the estimated 23 cents. The reported sales were 69.3 billion Krona, a 5% Y/Y decline, but beat the consensus revenue estimate of $7.03 billion. Organic sales rose by 6%. The Networks division had a 6% sales decrease, while the Enterprise segment fell by 25% due to divestment of iconectiv. However, this was balanced by a 3% rise in Cloud Software and Services sales. In the Networks segment, organic sales dropped by 4%. Europe, the Middle East, and...
